content:
First page of an untitled PDF. The protagonist moved through the unfamiliar landscape
with a quiet attentiveness, noting every shift in light and weather as if reading a
language she half understood. The path narrowed as it climbed, then widened again
where a small stream cut across it. She paused at the crossing to drink, cupping cold
water in her hands. The taste was clean and stony, almost mineral. She did not know yet
that the choice of which fork to take would shape the next several days. The map she
carried was old, drawn long before the floods of the previous spring, and several
landmarks had already moved or vanished entirely. By the time she reached the ridge,
the sun had dropped behind the higher peaks and the valley below sat in a soft blue
shadow. Lights began to appear in the windows of the houses scattered across the
